summary:
Pimavanserin is an FDA-approved medication specifically indicated for treating hallucinations and delusions associated with Parkinson’s disease dementia. It works by blocking serotonergic receptors involved in psychosis while minimizing adverse effects such as worsening tremor or rigidity that are common with other antipsychotics used in this population. This makes it particularly suitable due to its favorable safety profile compared to conventional agents like haloperidol which can worsen movement disorders. The available literature supports its efficacy and tolerability among patients suffering from PD-related psychosis. Thus, based upon current guidelines including those from Movement Disorder Society, pimavanserin remains recommended first-line therapy when considering pharmacological management options targeting these specific psychiatric manifestations within neurodegenerative conditions involving dopamine deficiency patterns typical seen across progressive stages of idiopathic Parkinson’s disease progression over time — emphasizing both effectiveness and patient-centered outcomes related directly back into daily functioning improvement through symptom control rather than merely suppressing behavioral issues alone but also improving quality-of-life metrics significantly during long-term follow-up periods observed clinically post-treatment initiation. 

Final Answer: Yes
